about.title: About Fch Catechesis
about.description1: FCH is a national organization committed to advancing evangelization and catechesis among individuals and communities involved in ministry, leadership, and faith formation at every level. Through mentorship, collaboration, education, and advocacy, we strive to strengthen and support those who dedicate themselves to sharing the Gospel and nurturing lifelong faith development.
about.description2: Rooted in the richness and diversity of the Hispanic/Latino community, FCH works to empower leaders, catechists, ministers, and families by providing guidance, resources, and opportunities for formation that respond to the cultural and spiritual needs of the people they serve.

webinar.title:

prayer.title: Prayer Of The Roman Missal
lines: O God, shepherd and ruler of all the faithful,
look favorably upon your servant, Leo XIV,
whom you have set at the head of your Church as her shepherd;
grant, we pray, that by word and example
he may be of service to those over whom he presides
so that, together with the flock entrusted to his care,
he may come to everlasting life.
prayer.closing:
Through our Lord Jesus Christ, your Son,
who lives and reigns with you in the unity of the Holy Spirit,
one God, for ever and ever. Amen.

items.text: The Church in the United States must have a space where religious educators and other pastoral leaders come together on a regular basis to reflect about how catechesis takes place in light of the Hispanic Catholic Experience.
items.name: Hosffman Ospino, Phd

items.text: Hispanic catechists need an organization to be able to share. What can we share? Well, our goals, achievements, successes, challenges and how we learn along the way.
items.name: Iupita Vital
items.role: Director to the Hispanic Apostolate, San Jose Diócesis, CA

items.text: FCH is a place to go and find a network of people with whom we can share what I do in catechism sessions.
items.name: Maria Garcia

items.text: An incredible resource that has transformed how we approach pastoral leadership in our parish community.
items.name: Rev. Anthony Smith

support.title: Become A Proud Fch Member
support.description: Together we can help each other be better catechists, 'a Christian who receives a particular calling from God that, when accepted in faith, empowers him for the service of transmission of the faith and for the task of initiating others into the Christian life' as describe by The Directory for Catechesis.
support.supportText: We are more than an organization or a website. We are a group of catechists like you who believe that when we share our teaching/evangelization best practices, we are stronger.

items.title: Augustine Institute Sponsors Fch At The La Conference
items.description: A big thank you to the Augustine Institute for sponsoring Rev. Yojaneider Garcia, FCH Theologian, to attend the Los Angeles.

items.description: May God's love and peace be a comfort to all those who knew, loved and were blessed by the ministry.
items.author: Luke Admin

hero.title: Partner With Us In Mission

fch.title: About FCH
fch.description1: The Federation for Catechesis with Hispanics is a national organization that promotes evangelization and catechesis for those who serve in catechetical ministry at every level.
fch.description2: FCH mentors and supports leaders who serve Hispanic/Latino communities by developing leaders, promoting new leadership, and identifying resources that strengthen catechists and catechetical ministry.
fch.historyTitle: Overview of the Federation for Catechesis with Hispanics (FCH) History
fch.historyDescription: The Federation for Catechesis with Hispanics (FCH) started as a Forum for Catechesis with Hispanics within NCCL, and later became the Federation for Catechesis with Hispanics (FCH). In 2022, FCH became an independent not-for-profit national organization.

mission.missionText: To Serve Those Who Minister In Catechesis With Hispanics.
mission.serveText1: FCH is here for those who serve, lead, and accompany Hispanic/Latino communities in faith formation and evangelization.
mission.serveText2: We support diocesan leaders, parish catechists, ministry volunteers, educators, organizations, and pastoral leaders at every level. Whether you are experienced in catechetical ministry or just beginning your journey, FCH provides support, guidance, resources, and a community committed to walking with you.

journey.title: Our Journey
journey.description1: Originally established as a collaborative Forum, FaithCommunity evolved through a shared vision of national unity. Recognizing the growing need for a dedicated, autonomous voice for Hispanic catechesis, we transitioned into an independent national organization.
journey.description2: This transition allowed us to expand our reach, formalize our leadership structures, and create more impactful partnerships with dioceses and educational institutions across the country.

approach.title: Our Professional Approach
approach.subtitle: A holistic methodology combining tradition with modern professional standards.

cards.title: Theological Foundations
cards.description: We ground our work in sound theological study, ensuring that all catechetical leaders are well-equipped with the intellectual tools required for teaching the faith.

cards.title: Cultural Competency
cards.description: Bridging languages and traditions with grace.

cards.title: Leadership Development
cards.description: Fostering administrative skills and strategic planning for church organizations.

cards.title: Spiritual Growth
cards.description: Formation isn't just professional; it's personal. We provide retreats and spiritual direction tailored for the needs of those in high-intensity ministry roles.

items.title: Enhance Mission
items.description: Working alongside the Church to deepen its impact in Hispanic communities.

items.title: Build Bridges
items.description: Fostering collaboration between various national and local organizations.

items.title: Share Resources
items.description: Creating a central hub for bilingual catechetical materials and research.

items.title: Advocate Inclusion
items.description: Ensuring Hispanic voices are represented at all levels of leadership.

history.pageDescription: Since our inception, FCH has been dedicated to serving those who minister in catechesis with Hispanics. Our timeline reflects years of commitment, faith, and community growth as we continue to empower leaders and catechists across the nation.
